0/0.label: RR — Power/sprint profile
0/0.interpretation: Two R alleles. Associated with greater fast-twitch (Type IIx) fibre density. Favours explosive, power-based, and sprint sports.

0/1.label: RX — Mixed profile
0/1.interpretation: One R allele and one stop-gain X allele. Balanced fast/slow-twitch distribution; no strong predisposition in either direction.

1/1.label: XX — Endurance profile
1/1.interpretation: Two X stop-gain alleles; α-actinin-3 protein is absent. Associated with greater Type I oxidative-fibre efficiency. Favours endurance events.

0/0.label: No ε4 at this locus
0/0.interpretation: T/T at rs429358. In combination with rs7412, consistent with ε2 or ε3 background. Not associated with elevated APOE-linked lipid or Alzheimer risk at this position alone.

0/1.label: One ε4 allele at this locus
0/1.interpretation: T/C at rs429358. One C allele is a component of the APOE ε4 haplotype. Full ε status requires combining with rs7412. Heterozygous ε3/ε4 is associated with moderately elevated LDL and cardiovascular risk.

1/1.label: Two ε4 alleles at this locus
1/1.interpretation: C/C at rs429358. Consistent with ε4/ε4 homozygosity (confirm with rs7412). Associated with significantly elevated LDL, higher cardiovascular risk, and increased lifetime risk of late-onset Alzheimer disease.

0/0.label: No ε2 at this locus
0/0.interpretation: C/C at rs7412. In combination with rs429358, consistent with ε3 or ε4 background. Full APOE ε genotype requires combining both SNPs.

0/1.label: One ε2 allele at this locus
0/1.interpretation: C/T at rs7412. One T allele is a component of the APOE ε2 haplotype. ε2 carriers typically show lower LDL; rare ε2/ε2 homozygosity carries elevated risk of type III hyperlipoproteinaemia.

1/1.label: Two ε2 alleles at this locus
1/1.interpretation: T/T at rs7412. Consistent with ε2/ε2 homozygosity (confirm with rs429358). Associated with lower LDL on average, but ε2/ε2 carries elevated risk of type III hyperlipoproteinaemia.

0/0.label: CC — Normal MTHFR activity
0/0.interpretation: No 677T variant. MTHFR enzyme activity is normal. Standard folate and B12 supplementation is appropriate.

0/1.label: CT — Mildly reduced MTHFR activity
0/1.interpretation: Heterozygous 677CT. Approximately 35% reduction in MTHFR enzyme activity. May benefit from methylated folate (5-MTHF) and methylcobalamin over synthetic folic acid.

1/1.label: TT — Significantly reduced MTHFR activity
1/1.interpretation: Homozygous 677TT. Approximately 70% reduction in MTHFR activity. Elevated homocysteine risk. Supplementing with methylated folate and methylcobalamin is commonly recommended; discuss with a healthcare provider.

0/0.label: AA — Normal MTHFR activity at this site
0/0.interpretation: No 1298C variant. This MTHFR site is unaffected. Impact is generally milder than C677T; consider combined status with rs1801133.

0/1.label: AC — Mildly reduced MTHFR activity
0/1.interpretation: Heterozygous 1298AC. Moderately reduced MTHFR activity at this site, especially in compound heterozygosity with C677T. Methylated folate supplementation may be beneficial.

1/1.label: CC — Moderately reduced MTHFR activity
1/1.interpretation: Homozygous 1298CC. Moderately reduced enzyme activity. Less severe than 677TT alone, but combined with any 677T variant can substantially impair methylation capacity.

0/0.label: FF — Standard VDR signalling
0/0.interpretation: Two F alleles. VDR protein is the shorter, more transcriptionally active form. Standard vitamin D response; typical supplementation thresholds apply.

0/1.label: Ff — Intermediate VDR signalling
0/1.interpretation: One F and one f allele. Intermediate VDR activity; modest supplementation adjustment is unlikely to be necessary without clinical guidance.

1/1.label: ff — Reduced VDR signalling
1/1.interpretation: Two f alleles. VDR protein is the longer, less transcriptionally active isoform. May require higher vitamin D3 intake to achieve equivalent serum 25(OH)D targets.

0/0.label: BB — Normal vitamin D response
0/0.interpretation: Homozygous B allele. Associated with adequate calcium absorption and normal bone mineral density response to vitamin D supplementation.

0/1.label: Bb — Intermediate response
0/1.interpretation: Heterozygous Bb. Intermediate vitamin D responsiveness; most individuals respond normally to standard supplementation.

1/1.label: bb — Attenuated vitamin D response
1/1.interpretation: Homozygous b allele. Associated with modestly reduced intestinal calcium absorption efficiency; may be relevant when optimising bone health and vitamin D protocol.

0/0.label: GG — Efficient fatty acid conversion
0/0.interpretation: Two G alleles. Higher FADS1 (δ5-desaturase) activity; efficient conversion of DGLA to arachidonic acid and ALA to EPA. Dietary ALA is relatively well converted.

0/1.label: GT — Intermediate conversion
0/1.interpretation: One G, one T allele. Intermediate FADS1 activity. Pre-formed EPA/DHA from oily fish or supplements remains the most reliable source.

1/1.label: TT — Reduced fatty acid conversion
1/1.interpretation: Two T alleles. Reduced FADS1 activity; less efficient conversion of short-chain omega-3 (ALA) to long-chain EPA/DHA. Pre-formed EPA/DHA supplementation is particularly relevant.

0/0.label: GG — Efficient FADS2 activity
0/0.interpretation: Two G alleles. Normal δ6-desaturase activity; efficient conversion of linoleic acid to DGLA and ALA to stearidonic acid.

0/1.label: GA — Intermediate FADS2 activity
0/1.interpretation: One G, one A allele. Modest reduction in FADS2 activity. Direct omega-3 sources (EPA/DHA) are preferable to relying solely on ALA conversion.

1/1.label: AA — Reduced FADS2 activity
1/1.interpretation: Two A alleles. Reduced δ6-desaturase activity; impaired elongation of short-chain fatty acids. Supplementing with pre-formed EPA/DHA rather than ALA (e.g., flaxseed oil) is recommended.

0/0.label: CC — Slow caffeine metaboliser
0/0.interpretation: Two C alleles. CYP1A2 induction is lower; caffeine is cleared more slowly. Higher plasma caffeine exposure per dose. Associated with elevated cardiovascular risk at high intake in some studies.

0/1.label: CA — Intermediate caffeine metaboliser
0/1.interpretation: One C, one A allele. Intermediate CYP1A2 induction. Caffeine clearance rate is between slow and fast phenotypes.

1/1.label: AA — Fast caffeine metaboliser
1/1.interpretation: Two A alleles. CYP1A2 is strongly inducible; caffeine is cleared quickly. Lower adverse cardiovascular risk at moderate intake. Late caffeine consumption may have less sleep impact than in slow metabolisers.

0/0.label: Normal CYP2D6 at *4 locus
0/0.interpretation: No *4 loss-of-function allele at this position. CYP2D6 activity at this locus is unimpaired. Note: full CYP2D6 phenotype requires testing multiple variants and copy-number variation.

0/1.label: One *4 allele — possible intermediate metaboliser
0/1.interpretation: One CYP2D6 *4 loss-of-function allele. Intermediate metaboliser status is possible. Drugs metabolised by CYP2D6 (codeine, tamoxifen, many antidepressants, beta-blockers) may have altered efficacy or tolerability.

1/1.label: Two *4 alleles — likely poor metaboliser
1/1.interpretation: Two CYP2D6 *4 loss-of-function alleles. Likely poor metaboliser status at this locus. Many CYP2D6-substrate drugs will have markedly reduced clearance; dose adjustment or drug substitution is often warranted — consult a pharmacist or physician.

0/0.label: Normal CYP2C19 at *2 locus
0/0.interpretation: No *2 loss-of-function allele. CYP2C19 activity is unimpaired at this site. Drugs metabolised by CYP2C19 (clopidogrel, omeprazole, certain antidepressants) are expected to process normally.

0/1.label: One *2 allele — intermediate metaboliser
0/1.interpretation: One CYP2C19 *2 loss-of-function allele. Intermediate metaboliser. Clopidogrel activation may be reduced; omeprazole exposure higher. Clinical significance depends on the drug and indication.

1/1.label: Two *2 alleles — poor metaboliser
1/1.interpretation: Two CYP2C19 *2 alleles. Poor metaboliser of CYP2C19 substrates. Clopidogrel has markedly impaired antiplatelet activation; alternative antiplatelet therapy is guideline-recommended. Discuss implications with your prescribing clinician.

0/0.label: Normal CYP2C9 at *2 locus
0/0.interpretation: No *2 variant. CYP2C9 activity is unimpaired at this position. Warfarin, NSAIDs, and other CYP2C9 substrates process normally from this locus.

0/1.label: One *2 allele — intermediate metaboliser
0/1.interpretation: One CYP2C9 *2 allele. Intermediate metaboliser. Warfarin dose requirements are typically lower; NSAID exposure may be higher. Monitor closely if starting anticoagulation.

1/1.label: Two *2 alleles — significantly reduced metabolism
1/1.interpretation: Two CYP2C9 *2 alleles. Substantially reduced CYP2C9 activity. Warfarin and other narrow-therapeutic-index drugs require careful dose reduction and monitoring. Consult a clinician before initiating affected medications.
